title: Git Essentials: Version Control for Frontend Developers
summary: Enable confident source code management with Git, focusing on workflows and collaboration needs of frontend engineers.
personalizationNote: You have Git basics; we’ll build on them to cover branching, rebasing, conflict resolution, and team collaboration practices.
learningObjectives: Use rebasing to maintain clean history
Collaborate using pull requests and code reviews
knowledgeChecks: What is one benefit of branching in Git?
Name a step in manually resolving merge conflicts.
Why use git rebase instead of merge?
narrationScript:
Scene 1: Branching and Merging Fundamentals
Create branches for features, switch context easily, and merge back once tested to maintain a stable main branch.

Scene 2: Resolving Merge Conflicts Gracefully
When Git flags conflicts, open affected files, analyze differences, and carefully merge code to unify changes without overwriting work.

Scene 3: Using Git Rebase for a Cleaner History
Use git rebase to integrate changes cleanly. Interactive rebasing lets you edit, squash, or reorder commits before sharing code.

Scene 4: Collaborating with Pull Requests and Code Reviews
Write clear PR descriptions, address reviewer comments constructively, and use reviews to learn and keep code solid.

slides.title: Branching and Merging Fundamentals
slides.speakerNotes: Branches let you develop features safely without disrupting main code. Clean merges keep history understandable and bugs minimal.
slides.narration: Create branches for features, switch context easily, and merge back once tested to maintain a stable main branch.
slides.visualCue: Terminal commands creating and merging branches

slides.title: Resolving Merge Conflicts Gracefully
slides.speakerNotes: Conflicts happen when multiple changes collide. Learning how to resolve them calmly avoids team delays and lost work.
slides.narration: When Git flags conflicts, open affected files, analyze differences, and carefully merge code to unify changes without overwriting work.
slides.visualCue: Sample conflicted file and resolution steps

slides.title: Using Git Rebase for a Cleaner History
slides.speakerNotes: Rebasing reorganizes commits to create a linear, easier to follow history — valuable for peer reviews and debugging.
slides.narration: Use git rebase to integrate changes cleanly. Interactive rebasing lets you edit, squash, or reorder commits before sharing code.
slides.visualCue: Visualized commit graph before and after rebasing

slides.title: Collaborating with Pull Requests and Code Reviews
slides.speakerNotes: Pull requests and code reviews enforce quality and knowledge sharing across frontend teams, vital for your role at Veyon.
slides.narration: Write clear PR descriptions, address reviewer comments constructively, and use reviews to learn and keep code solid.
slides.visualCue: Screenshot of GitHub PR with comments
